content.text: Orbit is a lightweight, distributed container orchestration system built in **Go**. It manages the lifecycle of containers across a cluster of worker nodes, handling scheduling, health checks, and state management.

content.title: Tech Stack
content.text: Built with **Go**, **Docker SDK**, and **RESTful APIs**. Uses a manager-worker architecture.

content.caption: Figure 1: The Manager-Worker Node Architecture. Click to Zoom.

content.text: Orbit follows a master-slave architecture similar to Kubernetes, but simplified for educational clarity.
The brain of the operation. It exposes an API for users to submit tasks.

items: **Scheduler**: Decides which node gets the task (supports Round Robin & Least Loaded).

content.text: Workers execute the actual tasks using the Docker SDK.
Orbit supports pluggable scheduling algorithms. The default is `RoundRobin`.

content.title: Design Choice
content.text: I chose Interfaces for the Scheduler to make it easy to swap algorithms (e.g., switch to 'Least Containers' strategy) without rewriting the core logic.
